title: The Ming Occupation
summary: In 1407, the failure of the Ho Dynasty fell our country into slavery under the Ming Dynasty with extremely brutal policies.

slides.text: Hey kids! After the brilliant Tran Dynasty, our country faced difficulties and was invaded by the Ming Dynasty from the North. They imposed very strict rules.
slides.imagePrompt: Enemy soldiers in dark armor building a heavy wall, people working with chains, oppressive atmosphere, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Ming invaders forced our people to pay heavy taxes, seizing all the gold, silver, treasures, and even the talented people of our country to take back to their land.
slides.imagePrompt: Carts loaded with treasures and books leaving a village, sad villagers watching, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: In particular, they tried every way to erase Vietnamese culture, burning all precious books and forcing our people to follow their customs. Life for the people was miserable.
slides.imagePrompt: A pile of scrolls being burned (symbolic, not scary), a child hiding a small Vietnamese book behind their back, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The pain of losing the country and the brutality of the invaders fueled the people's will to fight. Everyone was quietly preparing for a great uprising.
slides.imagePrompt: People talking secretly under the moon, eyes full of hope and fire, a hidden sword, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: When did the Ming Dynasty begin their occupation of our country?
Which brutal policy of the Ming invaders did our people hate the most?
options: Burning precious books and erasing Vietnamese culture
Teaching people how to plant flowers
slides.answer: Burning precious books and erasing Vietnamese culture
